signs: Roots filling the pot or growing out of drainage holes
Soil dries out faster than usual between waterings
Crowded crown with declining frond vigor
Plant has outgrown its container and needs division

maintenance.pruning: Remove old, dead, or browning fronds at soil level to encourage fresh growth; if the plant sheds heavily after drying out, fronds can be cut back to about 2 inches (5 cm) to regenerate.

nativeClimate.description: Native to humid tropical and subtropical forests, with a pantropical distribution spanning Florida, the West Indies, Mexico, Central and South America, Polynesia, and parts of Africa. It grows as an understory and epiphytic fern in warm, shaded, consistently moist and highly humid environments.
